claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 15 years old, and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 15 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! And the next question: **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Since Alex is 15 years old and lives with you in the UK, you would normally qualify for Child Benefit as you're responsible for a child under 16.

Just to complete the assessment, I have one more important question: **Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000?** 

(This won't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may mean you need to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I haven't mentioned anything about my income - is that something that affects whether I can get Child Benefit at all, or just how much I might have to pay back?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
You're absolutely right to clarify! Your income does **not** affect whether you're eligible to receive Child Benefit. 

The income question is only about the **High Income Child Benefit tax charge** - if you or your partner earn over £60,000, you can still claim and receive Child Benefit, but you may have to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

For the purpose of determining your **eligibility** to claim Child Benefit for Alex, I have all the information I need.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Since Alex is 15 years old, lives with you, and you're in the UK, you meet all the standard eligibility requirements. You're responsible for a child under 16, which means you qualify for Child Benefit.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Remember, eligibility will continue even after Alex turns 16, as long as he stays in approved education or training until he's under 20.

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for him. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 15-year-old son Alex. Since Alex is under 16 and lives with the claimant, the claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for him. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 15-year-old son Alex. Since Alex is under 16 and lives with the claimant, the claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
